content.text:
<user_query>
You are the Product execution lead for BIQc Sprint execution. Audit the repository for no-drop risk and sprint readiness against: route inventory, menu inventory, pricing/state parity, and module packaging assumptions. Use files under frontend/src/App.js, frontend/src/components/DashboardLayout.js, frontend/src/components/MobileNav.js, frontend/src/config/routeAccessConfig.js, memory/PLATFORM_TECHNICAL_BLUEPRINT.md, memory/ELT_BOARD_REGRESSION_REGISTER_2026.md. Return concise output: (1) top 12 no-drop risks, (2) must-pass success criteria for Sprint A and Sprint B, (3) explicit failure criteria, (4) guardrails that prevent drift, (5) required evidence artifacts list.
</user_query>
Auditing the repository by reading the specified files and related routing/pricing configuration.
Cross-checking `tierResolver`, `tiers.js`, and `pricingTiers.js` for entitlement parity with routes and nav.

Here is a concise audit tied to the files you named and the cross-checks above.

---

## (1) Top 12 no-drop risks

1. **`ROUTE_ACCESS_MAP` is not a full inventory of `App.js` routes** — Many authenticated paths are absent from `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\frontend\src\config\routeAccessConfig.js` (e.g. admin children, `/knowledge-base`, `/calibration-qa`, upgrade/subscribe paths, `/observability`). Comments in that file say it is the single source of truth, but coverage is partial.

2. **Unmapped routes default to “allowed” in tier checks** — In `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\frontend\src\lib\tierResolver.js`, `checkRouteAccess` returns `{ allowed: true }` when `getRouteAccess(route)` is null. That means sidebar lock logic (`canAccess` in `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\frontend\src\config\tiers.js`) does not enforce min-tier for paths that are not registered or prefix-matched.

3. **Prefix matching can mis-label nested URLs** — `getRouteAccess` uses `normalized.startsWith(route + '/')`. A path like `/market/calibration` (Forensic Calibration in `App.js`) inherits whatever config matches the longest applicable prefix; today `/market` is **free** in the map, so anything under `/market/...` may be treated like Market unless a more specific entry exists first. That is a **commercial / gating** risk if Forensic Calibration is not intended to be free-tier equivalent.

4. **`LaunchRoute` vs map: `access` is authoritative, `launchType` naming diverges** — Routes pass `access="paid"` or `waitlist` in `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\frontend\src\App.js`, while the map uses `launchType: 'foundation'` for the same commercial ideas. Redirects and `getLockedRedirect` in `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\frontend\src\components\DashboardLayout.js` key off `routeConfig.launchType`; inconsistency between **paid** and **foundation** labels can cause wrong upsell destination or messaging unless kept aligned.

5. **Waitlist vs “Foundation” commercial story** — `pricingTiers.js` / `TIER_FEATURES` in `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\frontend\src\config\pricingTiers.js` and `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\frontend\src\config\tiers.js` list starter features as if shipped, while `App.js` still wraps many modules in `LaunchRoute access="waitlist"`. That is a **PR-02 / CM-01** pricing-and-availability mismatch (register lines 16–17, 50–51).

6. **Desktop vs mobile nav inventory drift** — `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\frontend\src\components\MobileNav.js` builds `moreSections` dynamically; the static `MORE_SECTIONS` block is unused. The mobile “More” group omits items that appear in desktop’s broader sheets (e.g. Decision Tracker / Ingestion Audit appear in the unused constant but not in the live mobile “More” list), risking **UI-01** desktop/mobile parity failure.

7. **`resolveTier` maps `admin` role to `super_admin`** — In `tierResolver.js`, `role === 'admin'` yields `super_admin` tier. That can widen effective entitlement versus email-only `isPrivilegedUser` checks used in `LaunchRoute` for waitlist bypass, creating **AR-01 / SC-02** role-normalization drift if not mirrored on the backend.

8. **Calibration dependency vs repo state** — `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\memory\PLATFORM_TECHNICAL_BLUEPRINT.md` (B2) assumes Edge `calibration-psych` and related flows. Git status shows `supabase/functions/calibration_psych/index.ts` deleted; that is **DI-03** edge deploy / function inventory risk if production still expects that function.

9. **Catch-all route hides broken deep links** — `App.js` uses `<Route path="*" element={<Navigate to="/" replace />} />`, so missing routes silently go to marketing home instead of a controlled 404, weakening **PR-01** detection of route regressions.

10. **Preview routes in non-production** — `previewRoutesEnabled` is true when `NODE_ENV !== 'production'`, exposing preview routes without requiring `REACT_APP_ENABLE_PREVIEW_ROUTES`; increases surface area for accidental QA leakage.

11. **Blueprint promises vs implementation** — The blueprint’s backlog (e.g. half-built alert actions, automations backend, undeployed calibration edges) conflicts with “nothing dropped” narrative; **CM-04** “capability readiness” can fail if comms imply GA for waitlist or half-built modules.

12. **Regression register vs code reality** — `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\memory\ELT_BOARD_REGRESSION_REGISTER_2026.md` requires a canonical lock to `App.js` **and** `routeAccessConfig.js` (PR-01). Until the map is complete and tested, **gate evidence can pass documentation while runtime entitlement logic remains ambiguous** on unmapped paths.

---

## (2) Must-pass success criteria

**Sprint A (structure: inventory, entitlements, commercial parity)**

- **PR-01:** Every shipped customer-facing route in `App.js` is listed and classified in `routeAccessConfig.js` (or an explicitly documented exception list with owner sign-off); automated or manual diff shows **no orphan routes**.
- **AR-01 / PR-02:** One **entitlement + commercial parity matrix**: for each priced/waitlist feature, `LaunchRoute`, nav visibility, and pricing copy (`pricingTiers` / marketing) agree.
- **PR-05:** Waitlist routes deny non-privileged free users at the router (`LaunchRoute`) and show consistent lock behavior in nav (`getLockedRedirect` / `canAccess`).
- **DI-03:** Edge functions referenced by blueprint-critical paths (calibration, cognitive warmup, etc.) exist in deploy target and match **function naming** expectations (register AR-05).

**Sprint B (experience, governance evidence, release safety)**

- **PR-03:** Onboarding/calibration journey replay for free, paid, and admin personas completes without dead redirects (aligns with blueprint Part B).
- **UI-01 / UI-02:** Desktop `DashboardLayout` and `MobileNav` share the same **route/label dictionary** for primary journeys; loading/empty/error/locked patterns are consistent on high-traffic routes.
- **UX-02 / UX-03:** Failure and trust states present on cognition-heavy surfaces (per register UX-02/03).
- **DO-01 / DO-05:** Post-deploy probes for routes + edge warm path; **terminal visual evidence index** for the release (register DO-05).
- **CM-01:** GTM / pricing pages do not claim availability beyond the route matrix.

*(Sprint labels are inferred: A = registry + routing + commercial truth; B = journey UX + parity + deploy evidence. Adjust naming if your BIQc sprint charter defines A/B differently.)*

---

## (3) Explicit failure criteria

- Any **paid/foundation** feature reachable from pricing or nav for a **free** user without hitting `/biqc-foundation` or subscribe flow (PR-02, AR-01).
- Any **waitlist** surface behaving as **generally available** for non-privileged users (PR-05).
- **Route inventory diff** showing routes in `App.js` missing from `routeAccessConfig` without waiver (PR-01).
- **Desktop/mobile** primary nav missing the same top tasks for the same tier (UI-01).
- **Deploy** where frontend ships but referenced Edge functions are missing or renamed (DO-01, DI-03).
- **Calibration / first-value path** broken for a standard new user after login (PR-03).
- **Commercial** claim on Pricing / starter tier list includes modules that are waitlist-only in `App.js` (CM-01, PR-02).

---

## (4) Guardrails that prevent drift

- **Single registry discipline:** Treat `routeAccessConfig.js` as mandatory for every gated path; block merges that add `Route` without map entry (per register PR-01).
- **Pair `App.js` + map changes** in the same PR; require **featureKey** alignment for foundation upsell query strings.
- **Automated route inventory test:** extract paths from `App.js` (or a generated manifest) and diff to `ROUTE_ACCESS_MAP`.
- **Prefix-order rule:** longer paths registered before shorter prefixes, or forbid prefix inheritance for commercial ambiguity (addresses `/market/...` risk).
- **Nav source:** One label dictionary consumed by `DashboardLayout` and `MobileNav` (UI-01).
- **Tier checks:** Change `checkRouteAccess` policy so **unknown paths fail closed** or require explicit `free` registration (AR-01).
- **Environment:** Gate preview routes on `REACT_APP_ENABLE_PREVIEW_ROUTES` only, or document exception.
- **Regression register cadence:** Monthly outputs per `ELT_BOARD_REGRESSION_REGISTER_2026.md` (lines 66–69).

---

## (5) Required evidence artifacts list

From `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\memory\ELT_BOARD_REGRESSION_REGISTER_2026.md` and the audit above, collect at minimum:

| Artifact (register ID) | Purpose |
|------------------------|---------|
| No-drop inventory diff report (PR-01) | Route/nav vs `App.js` + map |
| Commercial parity matrix (PR-02, CM-01) | Pricing vs routes |
| Journey test log (PR-03) | Onboarding/calibration |
| Waitlist lock report (PR-05) | Waitlist enforcement |
| Desktop/mobile parity report (UI-01) | Nav alignment |
| State consistency / trust UX checklist (UI-02, UX-03) | Degraded and trust UI |
| Deploy parity report (DO-01) | FE/BE/edge alignment |
| Terminal evidence index (DO-05) | Visual/release proof |
| Entitlement parity matrix (AR-01) | FE vs BE entitlement |
| Edge deploy parity report (DI-03) | Functions deployed vs blueprint |
| GTM parity report (CM-01) | Marketing vs product |
| Capability readiness bulletin (CM-04) | What is actually GA vs waitlist |

Add for this codebase specifically: **prefix-match audit** for nested routes (e.g. `/market/calibration`), and **admin route certification** (SC-01) if admin children rely on `/admin` prefix matching.

---

**Files referenced:**  
`C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\frontend\src\App.js`, `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\frontend\src\components\DashboardLayout.js`, `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\frontend\src\components\MobileNav.js`, `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\frontend\src\config\routeAccessConfig.js`, `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\frontend\src\lib\tierResolver.js`, `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\frontend\src\config\tiers.js`, `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\frontend\src\config\pricingTiers.js`, `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\memory\PLATFORM_TECHNICAL_BLUEPRINT.md`, `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\memory\ELT_BOARD_REGRESSION_REGISTER_2026.md`.
